sections.paragraph:
σημεῖον, ου, τό [σῆμα ‘sign’] ‘an indirect way, as by a circumstance or deed, of indicating or verifying someth. at hand or in the future’, sign

—a. of a distinguishing mark

—α. a signal Mt 26:48; handwriting 2 Th 3:17.

—β. of indication or confirmation of a happening Mt 16:3; Mk 13:4; Lk 2:12; Ro 4:11; 1 Cor 14:22.

—b. someth. that confirms or validates through display of transcendent power

—α. of miracles: ascribed to God or Christ Mt 12:38f; 16:1; Mk 8:11f; Lk 11:16; J 2:11; 7:31; 11:47; 12:37; Ac 2:22; 7:36; 1 Cor 1:22; Hb 2:4; ascribed to apostles and other Christians Ac 2:43; 4:16; 5:12; 8:6; 14:3; 2 Cor 12:12b; ascribed to Satanic power or influence Mt 24:24; Mk 13:22; 2 Th 2:9; Rv 13:13f; 16:14; 19:20. As validation of authority 2 Cor 12:12a.

—β. portent Lk 21:11, 25; Ac 2:19; Rv 12:1, 3; 15:1.
